capability_statement: Users can judge an AOF release by capability first instead of inferring value from records, audits, schemas, dashboards, or tags.
before_state: AOF could require product value evidence, but release value was still not formalized as a versioned product capability model.
after_state: AOF has a product capability register, capability matrix, capability delta, and release audit that requires release notes to start from user capability.
scenario: A user opens v9.3 and wants to know in 30 seconds what changed from v9.2.
five_minute_demo: Open docs/aof-capability-matrix.md, read the v9.3 row, then run capability-first-release-audit.
time_saved_or_work_reduced: Users no longer need an AI-generated comparison just to understand the release claim.
cognitive_load_removed: Users do not need to translate audit and schema names into product capability manually.

capability_matrix.before: Product value evidence existed but capability modeling and delta were informal.
capability_matrix.after: Capability register, matrix, delta, and audit are required for release-ready claims.
capability_matrix.user_value: Release progress is readable as user capability before implementation mechanism.

capability_matrix.before: Value evidence could be present but still detached from a formal capability map.
capability_matrix.after: Value evidence is connected to product capability IDs and release acceptance criteria.
capability_matrix.user_value: Unclear release value becomes a product review trigger, not a documentation afterthought.

not_proven: This proves capability-first release governance, not market adoption or semantic correctness.
